comments.author: @debbiealleyne
comments.text: I am watching this video from Trinidad and Tobago 🇹🇹🇹🇹. I watched the Carbo Verde vs Argentina match and was very impressed with every player on the Carbo Verde National team. They expressed real teamwork on the field against the main player(s) on the Argentinian team. Excellent football Carbo Verde, the world wishes you well for a future FIFA World cup final🎉🎉 Congratulations to the goalkeeper Vozinha👏🏽👏🏽🎉🎉

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Hello beautiful Trinidad and Tobago! 🇹🇹🌊 Thank you for bringing that incredible Caribbean energy and football passion to the channel! You hit the nail on the head—Cabo Verde’s flawless teamwork, determination, and tactical grit against a global powerhouse like Argentina was absolute magic to watch. And yes, a massive shoutout to the legendary goalkeeper Vozinha! He was an absolute wall back there. 🧤⚽ Since you love great teamwork, what do you think was their most unforgettable tactical play of that entire historic match?

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: That’s a great practical question! 🌐 For an island nation, Cabo Verde actually has surprisingly decent 4G coverage and Wi-Fi in the main cities and tourist hubs like Sal and Praia, though it can get a bit spotty in the remote mountain villages. Are you researching for a potential remote-work trip or just curious about island infrastructure?"

comments.text: I am watching from Panama. I am an expat enjoying a better life than is now posissible to acheive in the US.  Free and honest elections for 50 years. I am impressed. Cabo Verde great place.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Greetings to Panama! 🇵🇦🌴 Thank you for sharing such a fascinating piece of your journey as an expat. Hearing your perspective on finding a better quality of life abroad, combined with your appreciation for Cape Verde’s own impressive democratic stability, is a wonderful insight! Authentic community and peace are truly becoming the ultimate modern luxuries. If you had to choose, what is the biggest cultural similarity you notice between Panama island life and Cape Verde?

comments.author: @FreeDerry
comments.text: The largest diaspora I the world is The Irish

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Slán! Thank you for bringing this fascinating piece of history to the thread! 🇮🇪☘️ The Irish diaspora is indeed legendary—with tens of millions of people worldwide tracing their roots back to the Emerald Isle, their global footprint and cultural impact are massive. It’s a powerful testament to resilience! What do you feel is the biggest cultural trait that Irish expats have successfully preserved across the world?

comments.author: @BruceEricGideonZabala
comments.text: Their so called "MORABEZA" also exists in the Philippine culture though we still don't have a specifically name for it due to our numerous dialects and sub-languages from Luzon, the Visayas and Mindanao

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Mabuhay! This is an absolute gold-standard cultural connection! 🇵🇭✨ It is fascinating to learn how the spirit of warmth and legendary hospitality analogous to 'Morabeza' breathes through the diverse cultures of Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao. Even without one single word for it, that shared human value is beautiful. Since you know both cultures so well, what is a specific welcoming gesture or tradition in the Philippines that feels exactly like Morabeza to you?

comments.author: @advgecko
comments.text: Watching from Patagonia Argentina 9:20pm. (-1°c outside) Cabo Verde certainly made us suffer at the match!! Glad we won but also great to see how well Cabo Verde played!! Nice to see companionship at the end of the game and great respect for the players. Landed on this video looking for info on the islands and their culture, looks like a lovely place with nice people...All the best for the future!

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: That is a brilliant comparison! 🌴☀️ Having been to the Canary Islands, you already know the magic of island life, so it's amazing that our video helped put Cabo Verde’s unique archipelago layout on the map for you. Their culture, especially the food and the rhythm of the nightlife, is truly infectious. If you do make that dream trip happen someday, are you more of a beach-relaxing traveler or an festival-explorer type?

replies.author: @advgecko
replies.text:
​@reallifeincountriesI didn't really know exactly where it was on the map. Nice to learn it's made up by several islands. I've been to the Canary Islands a couple of times and I saw an alike in some way...I think the things you mentioned in the video are interesting, how they live, their food, nightlife, carnaval, etc.. I would love to visit some day!!
Thanks

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Greetings to Patagonia! 🇦🇷 -1°C sounds freezing, but we hope our video brought some warmth! ❄️🔥 Cape Verde definitely gave Argentina a massive run for their money—that post-game respect between players was pure class. Since you're looking into their culture now, what's the number one thing about the islands that surprised you the most?"

comments.author: @MichelHolland-1994
comments.text:
Cabo Verde is NOTthe smallest country to have ever participated in the World Cup Football. That distinction belongs to Curaçao, a country within the Kingdom of the Netherlands—much like Scotland is within the UK.
It is a Caribbean island located north of Venezuela. It has a population of approximately 150,000 and an area of ​​444 km². Regards from The Netherlands.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: "Hartelijke groeten to The Netherlands! 🇳🇱 Thank you so much for this masterclass in football geography! You are completely right—Curaçao holds that incredible distinction, and the comparison of its status within the Kingdom to Scotland within the UK is a fantastic way to explain it. We love having such knowledgeable viewers keeping our facts tight. Are you a big follower of CONCACAF and African football, or just a passionate trivia fan?"

comments.text: Much love from Indonesia. So proud of your footballers team Cabo Verde🎉

replies.author: @RidaSaragih-q8r
replies.text: ​@reallifeincountriesNo. I don't. What makes us so sad and depressed is our football in Indonesia seems like it's used for politics electoral and only for some benefits of certain people or group. Even the long story of the federation corruption , poor leage governance, the power of insiders, and match fixing made it even worse. I think not only in Indonesia, most of countries experienced the same. But they learned and change. But I hope it will be better. You must be proud of your country. Cabo Verde is the new comer in the World Cup round but shocked the worldwide 🎉

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Vozinha was an absolute wall! 🧤⚽ Keeping a cool head and making those incredible, world-class saves truly made him a tournament legend. He definitely earned the respect of fans worldwide! And we love your dream for World Cup 2030—with the incredible passion for football in Indonesia, seeing the Garuda squad step onto the global stage would be absolutely magical! Do you feel Indonesian football is currently on the right track to make that 2030 qualification happen?

replies.author: @RidaSaragih-q8r
replies.text: I did. Actually I really like all of them and the way they play as a strong and solid team. But the goal keeper Vozinha wins my heart and the world. See you in 4 years Cabo Verde, come stronger and I hope my country Indonesia also can reach the ticket and qualification in World Cup 2030. 🎉

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Much love right back to Indonesia! 🇮🇩 Truely appreciate the warm support. Cabo Verde's team really did make a historic statement on the pitch. Did you have a favorite player or moment during their incredible run?
